listing.title: 2BR Downtown, steps from SkyTrain, only $900/mo (URGENT)
listing.description: Hi dear! I'm out of country for work. Pay refundable deposit to reserve. No viewing now. WhatsApp only +91-99999-00000. Wire transfer ok. Move in tomorrow.

signals.label: Requests deposit to reserve before viewing
signals.why_it_matters: Paying before verifying identity and seeing the unit is the key failure mode in most rental scams.

signals.label: Refuses an in-person viewing
signals.why_it_matters: Refusing a viewing is a strong scam indicator, especially when paired with deposit requests.

signals.label: Pushes WhatsApp-only communication
signals.why_it_matters: Scammers often move conversations off-platform to avoid moderation and tracking.

signals.label: Claims to be out of country
signals.why_it_matters: A common scam script: landlord is away so you can't verify the unit in person.

signals.label: Requests wire transfer
signals.why_it_matters: Wire transfers are hard to reverse and frequently used in scams.

signals.label: Price is far below typical range for the area/type
signals.why_it_matters: Scam listings often use unusually low rent to create urgency and extract a deposit before a viewing.
signals.evidence: Baseline (VANCOUVER UNIT BR=2): mean≈3350, sd≈650. Observed=900, z=-3.77

signals.label: Refundable deposit / money-back guarantee
signals.why_it_matters: A persuasion pattern used to lower suspicion before taking a deposit.

signals.label: High pressure / urgency language
signals.why_it_matters: Scammers pressure victims into paying quickly.

signals.label: Account looks very new
signals.why_it_matters: New accounts can be a sign of throwaway identities used for scams.

signals.label: Photo looks reused from other listings
signals.why_it_matters: Scammers often reuse attractive photos across multiple fake listings.

signals.label: High posting rate
signals.why_it_matters: High-volume posting can indicate spam or a scam campaign.

signals.label: Many people were denied or ignored
signals.why_it_matters: A pattern of rejected inquiries can suggest a bait-and-switch or fee harvesting.

signals.label: Photo looks reused from other listings
signals.why_it_matters: Scammers often reuse attractive photos across multiple fake listings.

topReasons: Requests deposit to reserve before viewing
Claims to be out of country
recommendedActions: Do not send any deposit or e-Transfer before an in-person viewing.
Insist on a verifiable landlord identity and proof of right-to-rent (matching name + ownership/management).
If off-platform contact is pushed (WhatsApp/Telegram), keep everything on-platform or walk away.
Reverse-image search the photos and check if the same listing appears elsewhere at a different price.
redundancySteps: Do an in-person viewing (or live video tour) before sending any money.
Ask for the landlord/agent’s full name and verify ownership (land registry / property manager website).
Reverse-image search 2–3 listing photos to spot duplicates across cities.
Compare rent vs nearby similar units (same beds/baths) to detect bait pricing.
Never pay via e-Transfer, crypto, gift cards, or cash before the lease is signed and keys are verified.
If you have a lease draft, run it through the Lease Simplifier to flag predatory clauses.
